children.text: There are already several excellent libraries with a functional flavor. Typically, they are meant

rects.text: There are already several excellent libraries with a functional flavor. Typically, they are meant

children.text: to be general-purpose toolkits, suitable for working in multiple paradigms. Ramda has a more

rects.text: to be general-purpose toolkits, suitable for working in multiple paradigms. Ramda has a more

children.text: focused goal. We wanted a library designed specifically for a functional programming style,

rects.text: focused goal. We wanted a library designed specifically for a functional programming style,

children.text: one that makes it easy to create functional pipelines, one that never mutates user data.

rects.text: one that makes it easy to create functional pipelines, one that never mutates user data.

Using Ramda should feel much like just using JavaScript.
It is practical, functional JavaScript. We’re not introducing
lambda

Using Ramda should feel much like just using JavaScript.
It is practical, functional JavaScript. We’re not introducing
lambda

expressions in strings, we’re not borrowing consed

native features of JavaScript, such as functions as objects
with properties.
